note: National and community/identity scholarships are curated from publicly known programs. Deadlines and amounts shift each cycle — always verify at the sponsor's official website. Field-specific scholarships are generated by AI per major selection and are NOT web-verified in real time.

national.name: Gates Scholarship
national.eligibility: Minority students with Pell Grant eligibility, GPA 3.3+, demonstrated leadership

national.name: Dell Scholars Program
national.eligibility: Pell Grant eligible, 2.4+ GPA, first-generation or low-income

national.name: QuestBridge National College Match
national.eligibility: High-achieving, low-income high school seniors; family income under $65,000

national.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
national.eligibility: High school seniors with leadership, service, and academic excellence; US citizen or PR

national.name: Horatio Alger Association Scholarship
national.eligibility: Students who have faced significant adversity; 2.0+ GPA; US citizen

communityIdentity.name: Hispanic Scholarship Fund (HSF)
communityIdentity.eligibility: Hispanic/Latino heritage; 3.0+ GPA; US citizen or PR; financial need

communityIdentity.name: United Negro College Fund (UNCF) Scholarships
communityIdentity.eligibility: African American students; financial need; GPA varies by program

communityIdentity.name: Point Foundation Scholarship
communityIdentity.amount: Up to full cost of attendance
communityIdentity.eligibility: LGBTQ+ students with strong academic record, leadership, and financial need

communityIdentity.name: First-Generation Foundation Scholarship
communityIdentity.eligibility: First-generation college students with financial need and academic promise

communityIdentity.name: Knights of Columbus Pro Deo & Pro Patria Scholarship
communityIdentity.eligibility: Catholic students at Catholic colleges; financial need; US or Canadian citizen
